headline: Sikh Community's 78 Gurdwaras Face Same Land Title Issues as Temples
context: The Malaysian Gurdwaras Council reported that 52 of 78 gurdwaras in Malaysia lack formal land titles, facing the same vulnerability to demolition as Hindu temples.

cards.big: 52 of Malaysia's 78 gurdwaras lack formal land titles.
cards.sub: Like Hindu temples, many were built on estate or government land before independence and never regularised.
Each demolition displaced a community with roots spanning 4-5 generations.

cards.big: Sikhs comprise 0.3% of Malaysia's population — approximately 100,000 people.
cards.sub: As a micro-minority, the community has minimal political leverage for land regularisation.

cards.big: The Cabinet Committee on Non-Muslim Places of Worship has no Sikh representation.
cards.sub: Decisions affecting Sikh religious sites are made without Sikh participation.

cards.big: A community present since the 1800s whose places of worship remain legally precarious. Longevity alone does not grant security.
Non-Muslim religious sites need a blanket regularisation exercise — not case-by-case negotiation. A one-time audit and titling programme would resolve a century of administrative neglect.
